---
title: "The datey specification"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{The datey specification}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>"
)
```

```{r setup}
library(datey)
```

## Introduction

The **datey** system is defined such that it can be used safely on a 
cross-platform basis.

## Types

There are two types:

- `datey` represents a date in the calendar years 1000 to 2999 inclusive.
- `durationy` represents a duration with magnitude 2000 years or less.

Both of the above types map to 32-bit two's complement integers, which we'll
call *clicks*.

## Fixed precision

The **datey** system uses an annual system where one year is represented as
534&#x202F;360 clicks.
